Based on the AI model's prediction,
The fact that there is no checking account, because general scoring systems rely on financial features to classify risk, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [1]
The fact that the loan duration is considered, because standard predictive models rely heavily on financial ratios, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [2]
The fact that the checking account balance is negative, because negative balances typically indicate liquidity constraints, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a low risk of default.
